title: Crunchy Fermented Japanese Noodle Dish
description: A kid-friendly, vegan-friendly Japanese dish featuring vermicelli rice noodles, crispy fried shallots, and fermented umeboshi plum sauce. This crunchy noodle dish is perfect for a quick and easy lunch or dinner.
instructions: Cook the vermicelli rice noodles according to package instructions and set aside.
In a blender or food processor, blend the umeboshi plums, rice vinegar, soy sauce, sesame oil, grated ginger, salt, and black pepper to make the fermented sauce.
Heat about 1-2 inches of oil in a deep frying pan to 350°F (175°C).
Fry the thinly sliced shallots in batches until crispy and golden brown, about 2-3 minutes per batch.
Remove the fried shallots from the oil with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked noodles, fermented sauce, and crispy fried shallots.
Toss everything together until the noodles are well coated with the sauce and the fried shallots are evenly distributed.
Garnish with additional umeboshi plums, grated ginger, or sesame seeds if desired.
